text: A gel point port is inserted through a 3.5 cm umbilical incision, followed by the insertion of a 2 mm mini lap needle scopic grasper into the right lower quadrant. The surgeon performs primary vascular control with complete mesocolic excision and central vascular ligation along the right border of the superior mesenteric vein. An avascular surgical plane along Toldt's line is exposed, revealing the duodenum.

The intraoperative view shows several enlarged lymph nodes in the right colic mesentery near the central vascular trunk. The surgeon performs lymph node dissection along the superior mesenteric artery and vein. The iliocolic vein is ligated and transected at its root, and the iliocolic artery is transected and dissected upwards towards the middle colic vessels.

The surgeon exposes an avascular surgical plane to reveal the head of the pancreas and duodenum. Lymph node dissection is performed around the middle colic vessels, followed by identification, ligation, and transection of the right branch of the middle colic artery at its root. The peritoneum in the retrocecal recess is incised and mobilized laterally. The greater omentum of the transverse colon is transected, and its attachment at the hepatic flexure is detached.

The final intraoperative view shows the surgical field after complete mesocolic excision and central vascular ligation for right-sided colon cancer. A small, two-millimeter incision is visible in the right lower quadrant, used for drainage placement.
